segments.speaker: Banker
segments.text: Hi, what brings you in today?

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: I'd like to open a checking account. I just moved here for graduate school.

segments.speaker: Banker
segments.text: Sure. Do you have a passport and proof of address?

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: I have my passport. For address, I only have temporary housing right now.

segments.speaker: Banker
segments.text: A lease or official school document may work, depending on the account.

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: I have my admission letter and a housing confirmation email. Would either of those help?

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: Thanks. I also wanted to ask about debit cards and international transfers.

segments.speaker: Banker
segments.text: We can issue a debit card, and we can explain wire transfer fees.

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: Great. I'm trying to understand the fees before I commit to anything.

segments.speaker: Banker
segments.text: Absolutely. We'll go through them line by line.

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: I appreciate that. Banking terms are still new to me in English.
